possible_causes.solution: Calibrate accelerometer in Betaflight Setup tab

possible_causes.cause: Radio not bound or no signal
possible_causes.solution: Bind receiver to transmitter, check Receiver tab in Betaflight
Set up an Arm mode in Betaflight Modes tab with a switch
Push throttle stick all the way down before arming

diagnostic_steps: Open Betaflight CLI and type 'status' — it shows exactly why arm is blocked
Check Receiver tab — move sticks and verify channel inputs respond
Check accelerometer calibration in Setup tab

symptoms: Rolls to one side on liftoff

possible_causes.solution: Verify motor spin directions in Betaflight Motors tab — standard X: FL=CCW, FR=CW, RL=CW, RR=CCW
CW props on CW motors, CCW props on CCW motors — check markings on props
Set correct yaw offset in Betaflight Configuration tab

diagnostic_steps: Remove props, spin motors in Betaflight — verify spin direction matches layout
Check prop markings match motor spin direction
Verify FC is facing forward in Betaflight (horizon should tilt correctly)

possible_causes.solution: Check ESC signal wire connection to FC, verify ESC protocol in Betaflight
Run ESC calibration: set throttle to max, connect battery, wait for beeps, lower throttle

diagnostic_steps: Count the beep pattern — each ESC brand has specific codes
Verify signal wire connections between ESC and FC

problem: FPV video has static or interference

possible_causes.cause: VTX and goggles on different channels
possible_causes.solution: Match VTX and goggle channel/band exactly (e.g., Raceband CH3)
Check antenna connector, replace if damaged
Add capacitor to VTX power, route VTX wires away from motor wires

diagnostic_steps: Verify goggles are on exact same band and channel as VTX
Test at very close range — if clean up close, it's a signal/power issue at range
